children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Architect of the Capitol Inspector General Act of 2007”.
conduct and supervise audits and investigations relating to the Architect of the Capitol;
provide leadership and coordination and recommend policies to promote economy, efficiency, and effectiveness; and

provide a means of keeping the Architect of the Capitol and the Congress fully and currently informed about problems and deficiencies relating to the administration of programs and operations of the Architect of the Capitol.

children.heading: Appointment of Inspector General; supervision; removal; pay; limits on bonuses; counsel
children.label: c Appointment of Inspector General; supervision; removal; pay; limits on bonuses; counsel

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There shall be at the head of the Office of Inspector General, an Inspector General who shall be appointed by the Architect of the Capitol, in consultation with the Inspectors General of the Library of Congress, Government Publishing Office, Government Accountability Office, and United States Capitol Police. The appointment shall be made without regard to political affiliation and solely on the basis of integrity and demonstrated ability in accounting, auditing, financial analysis, law, management analysis, public administration, or investigations. The Inspector General shall report to, and be under the general supervision of, the Architect of the Capitol.

children.heading: Audits, investigations, reports, and other duties and responsibilities
children.label: B Audits, investigations, reports, and other duties and responsibilities
i
children.text: initiating, carrying out, or completing any audit or investigation;

children.label: ii
children.text: issuing any subpoena during the course of any audit or investigation;

children.label: iii
children.text: issuing any report; or

children.label: iv
children.text: carrying out any other duty or responsibility of the Inspector General under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Inspector General may be removed from office, or transferred to another position within, or another location of, the Office of the Architect of the Capitol, by the Architect of the Capitol.

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on House Administration and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Rules and Administration and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: C Applicability
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall prohibit a personnel action (except for removal or transfer) that is otherwise authorized by law.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 3 Compensation
children.text: The Inspector General shall be paid at an annual rate of pay equal to $1,500 less than the annual rate of pay of the Architect of the Capitol.

children.heading: No bonuses
children.label: 4 No bonuses
children.text: The Inspector General may not receive any cash award or cash bonus, including a cash award under chapter 45 of title 5.

children.heading: Counsel
children.label: 5 Counsel
children.text: The Inspector General shall, in accordance with applicable laws and regulations governing selections, appointments, and employment at the Office of the Architect of the Capitol, obtain legal advice from a counsel reporting directly to the Inspector General or another Inspector General.

children.heading: Duties, responsibilities, authority, and reports
children.label: d Duties, responsibilities, authority, and reports
A
children.text: “Office of the Architect of the Capitol” for “establishment”; and

children.label: B
children.text: “Architect of the Capitol” for “head of the establishment”.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Inspector General, in carrying out this section, is authorized, without the supervision or approval of any other employee, office, or other entity within the Office of the Architect of the Capitol, to select, appoint, and employ such officers and employees (including consultants) as may be necessary for carrying out the functions, powers, and duties of the Office of Inspector General subject to the provisions of law governing selections, appointments, and employment in the Office of the Architect of the Capitol.

children.heading: Security and suitability
children.label: B Security and suitability
children.text: Appointments under the authority under subparagraph (A) shall be made consistent with personnel security and suitability requirements.

children.heading: Consultants
children.label: C Consultants
children.text: Any appointment of a consultant under the authority under subparagraph (A) shall be made consistent with section 6(a)(8) of the Inspector General Act of 1978 (5 U.S.C. App.).1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: make an arrest without a warrant while engaged in official duties as authorized under this section or any other statute for any offense against the United States committed in the presence of such supervisory special agent or special agent, or for any felony cognizable under the laws of the United States if such supervisory special agent or special agent has reasonable grounds to believe that the person to be arrested has committed or is committing such felony;

children.label: ii
children.text: seek and execute warrants for arrest, search of a premises, or seizure of evidence issued under the authority of the United States upon probable cause to believe that a violation has been committed; and

children.label: iii
children.text: carry a firearm while engaged in official duties as authorized under this section or any other statute.

children.label: aa
children.text: is a citizen of the United States;

children.label: bb
children.text: has successfully completed a basic law enforcement training program or military or other equivalent; and

children.label: cc
children.text: is not prohibited from receiving a firearm under Federal law, including under , because of a conviction of a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence.section 922(g)(9) of title 18

children.heading: Additional requirements
children.label: II Additional requirements
children.text: After providing notice to the appropriate committees of Congress, the Inspector General may add requirements to the certification required under subclause (I), as determined appropriate by the Inspector General.

children.heading: Maintenance of requirements
children.label: ii Maintenance of requirements
children.text: The Inspector General shall maintain firearms-related requirements (including quarterly firearms qualifications) and use of force training requirements that, except to the extent the Inspector General determines necessary to effectively carry out the duties of the Office of the Inspector General, are in accordance with the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency use of force policies, which incorporate Department of Justice guidelines.

children.label: aa
children.text: determine whether an individual meets the requirements under this paragraph; and

children.label: bb
children.text: revoke any authority granted to an individual under subparagraph (A) if the individual is not in compliance with the requirements of this paragraph.

children.heading: Reauthorization
children.label: II Reauthorization
children.text: The Inspector General may reauthorize an individual to exercise the authority granted under subparagraph (A) if the Inspector General determines the individual has achieved compliance with the requirements under this paragraph.

children.heading: Limitation on appeal
children.label: III Limitation on appeal
children.text: A revocation of the authority granted under subparagraph (A) shall not be subject to administrative, judicial, or other review, unless the revocation results in an adverse action. Such an adverse action may, at the election of the applicable individual, be reviewed in accordance with the otherwise applicable procedures.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Before the first grant of authority under subparagraph (A), and semiannually thereafter as part of the report under section 5 of the Inspector General Act of 1978 (5 U.S.C. App.), the Inspector General shall submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a written certification that adequate internal safeguards and management procedures exist that, except to the extent the Inspector General determines necessary to effectively carry out the duties of the Office of the Inspector General, are in compliance with standards established by the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency, which incorporate Department of Justice guidelines, to ensure proper exercise of the powers authorized under this paragraph.1

children.heading: Suspension of authority
children.label: ii Suspension of authority
children.text: The authority granted under this paragraph (including any grant of authority to an individual under subparagraph (A), without regard to whether the individual is in compliance with subparagraph (B)) may be suspended by the Inspector General if the Office of Inspector General fails to comply with the reporting and review requirements under clause (i) of this subparagraph or subparagraph (D). Any suspension of authority under this clause shall be reported to the appropriate committees of Congress.

children.heading: Peer review
children.label: D Peer review
children.text: To ensure the proper exercise of the law enforcement powers authorized under this paragraph, the Office of Inspector General shall submit to and participate in the external review process established by the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency for ensuring that adequate internal safeguards and management procedures continue to exist. Under the review process, the exercise of the law enforcement powers by the Office of Inspector General shall be reviewed periodically by another Office of Inspector General or by a committee of Inspectors General. The results of each review shall be communicated in writing to the Inspector General, the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency, and the appropriate committees of Congress.

children.heading: Alleged misconduct
children.label: E Alleged misconduct
children.text: Any allegation of misconduct by an individual granted authority under subparagraph (A) may be reviewed by the Integrity Committee of the Council of the Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency.

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on Rules and Administration and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on House Administration and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Budget independence
children.label: 4 Budget independence
children.text: The Architect of the Capitol shall include the annual budget request of the Inspector General in the budget of the Office of the Architect of the Capitol without change.

children.heading: Transfers
children.label: e Transfers
children.text: All functions, personnel, and budget resources of the Office of the Inspector General of the Architect of the Capitol as in effect before the effective date of this section are transferred to the Office of Inspector General described under subsection (b).

children.heading: References
children.label: f References
children.text: References in any other Federal law, Executive order, rule, regulation, or delegation of authority, or any document of or relating to the Inspector General of the Architect of the Capitol shall be deemed to refer to the Inspector General as set forth under this section.

children.heading: First appointment
children.label: g First appointment
children.text: By the date occurring 180 days after , the Architect of the Capitol shall appoint an individual to the position of Inspector General of the Architect of the Capitol described under subparagraph (A) of subsection (c)(1) in accordance with that subparagraph.December 26, 2007

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (2), this section shall take effect 180 days after , and apply with respect to fiscal year 2008 and each fiscal year thereafter.December 26, 2007

children.heading: First appointment
children.label: 2 First appointment
children.text: Subsection (g) shall take effect on , and the Architect of the Capitol shall take such actions as necessary after , to carry out that subsection.December 26, 2007December 26, 2007
